novel_features.name: Today View
novel_features.description: See all your issues for today across every team, ranked by priority and cycle deadline.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a compound local-store join across assignments, cycles, priorities, states, and relations that no single Linear API call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is asked 'what should I work on right now?', this returns the ranked list in one call instead of N team-scoped list queries.

novel_features.name: Bottleneck Detection
novel_features.description: See which team members are overloaded and which issues are blocked before sprint planning.
novel_features.rationale: Joins issues, assignees, cycle data, and dependency chains that only exist together in the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pre-sprint-planning question 'who is overloaded right now' becomes a single agent call instead of scrolling N tabs.

novel_features.name: Project Burndown
novel_features.description: Project a project's landing date by linear-regressing remaining estimate against the team's measured velocity.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local estimate and state time-series data plus per-cycle velocity history that isn't exposed as a single Linear endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces static project target-dates with a velocity-driven projection an agent can compare against the milestone date.

novel_features.name: Cycle Comparison
novel_features.description: Side-by-side metrics between any two cycles: completion %, scope added, scope cut, carryover, average cycle time.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-cycle comparison requires historical cycle snapshots stored locally; Linear's UI only shows the active cycle.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Friday-update ritual: 'how does this cycle compare to last cycle?' becomes one call.

novel_features.name: Stale Issue Radar
novel_features.description: Find issues that haven't been touched in N days, grouped by team and project.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local updatedAt tracking across the full issue corpus; doing this against the API on every call burns the complexity budget.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Backlog-grooming workflow: surface zombie issues without paying API complexity for a full scan.

novel_features.name: Slipped Issues
novel_features.description: Show what carried over from last cycle into this cycle, grouped by team and reason heuristic.
novel_features.rationale: Requires diffing the issue.cycle field across cycle-history rows; the API gives you the current state but not the rollover semantic.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Maya's Friday update needs 'what slipped' as a structured list, not a manual count.

novel_features.name: Blocking Queue
novel_features.description: Show issues you are blocking — sorted by impact (downstream count and priority).
novel_features.rationale: Linear shows the blocking relation per-issue but not as a personal queue; the answer requires a graph walk over the local issue_relation table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Daily ritual: 'what's blocked because of me' becomes one call instead of clicking through every assigned issue.

novel_features.name: Duplicate Detection
novel_features.description: Find issues that look like duplicates of a query string using offline FTS5 fuzzy matching.
novel_features.rationale: Requires SQLite FTS5 across all synced issue text; impossible offline with the GraphQL API alone.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Triage and grooming: catch dupes before filing or while sweeping the inbox.

novel_features.name: Velocity Trends
novel_features.description: Track sprint completion rates over the last N cycles to spot productivity trends.
novel_features.rationale: Requires historical cycle snapshots stored locally across multiple sprints; Linear's UI shows a single cycle at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Multi-cycle trend lines feed the burndown projection and the weekly stakeholder update.

novel_features.name: Initiatives Health
novel_features.description: Rolled-up portfolio view per initiative: child project progress, milestone target-vs-projected dates, slippage flags.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-entity rollup over initiatives → projects → milestones; competitor CLIs all stop at the project level.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Portfolio review: 'which milestone in my portfolio is most at risk this month' becomes one ranked answer.

novel_features.name: Test Fixture Lifecycle
novel_features.description: List Linear issues this CLI created in the current or named session, then archive them with pp-cleanup.
novel_features.rationale: The local pp_created table records every issue ID returned from issues create; cleanup invokes the real archive endpoint per row, scoped to fixtures the CLI itself made.
novel_features.example: linear-pp-cli pp-test list --session current && linear-pp-cli pp-cleanup --session current --dry-run
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets agents create test issues during a session and clean up only their own fixtures, never touching pre-existing tickets.

novel_features.name: Trust Mode Mutation Guard
novel_features.description: Refuse mutations on Linear issues not in the local pp_created ledger when --trust-mode strict is set; works on the create command and any future mutation surfaces.
novel_features.rationale: Pre-mutation client-side guard backed by the same pp_created table; floor against accidental mutation of pre-existing tickets in shared workspaces.
novel_features.example: linear-pp-cli issues create --title "test" --team ENG --trust-mode strict --session demo
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent runs against a real workspace, this is the safety net that prevents silent damage to non-test data.

narrative.headline: Every Linear feature, plus offline sync, FTS search, and cross-entity views no other Linear tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: linear-pp-cli wraps Linear's full GraphQL surface (issues, projects, cycles, milestones, initiatives, comments, documents) and adds a local SQLite store with FTS5 search, cycle history, and velocity snapshots. Compound commands like `today`, `bottleneck`, `cycles compare`, and `projects burndown` answer the Monday-morning sprint-planning and Friday-stakeholder-update questions in a single call.
narrative.auth_narrative: Linear authenticates with a Personal API Key in the `Authorization` header (no `Bearer` prefix). Set `LINEAR_API_KEY=lin_api_...` or run `linear-pp-cli auth login` to walk through it. The CLI is rate-limit aware — Linear measures complexity points (~1,500/hour for personal keys), so sync uses shallow Connection queries by default and pages cleanly.

quickstart.comment: Set your LINEAR_API_KEY for the session and verify viewer access.
Pull issues, cycles, projects, teams, users, labels, and states into the local SQLite store.
Cross-team, ranked-by-priority view of your work for the day.
Issues nobody has touched in a month, grouped by team and project.
Two-cycle diff for the Friday stakeholder update.
Project landing date projected from velocity.

troubleshoots.fix: Check `linear-pp-cli auth status`; rotate the key in linear.app/settings/api if needed and re-export `LINEAR_API_KEY`.
Sync uses shallow queries by default; if you've passed `--depth`, lower it. Linear caps personal keys around 1,500 complexity points/hour.

troubleshoots.symptom: FTS search returns no rows after sync
troubleshoots.fix: Run `linear-pp-cli sync --full` once after upgrading; v3 reprints rebuild the FTS index on first sync.
Either run `linear-pp-cli config set trust-mode normal`, or pass an issue ID this CLI created in the current session (see `linear-pp-cli pp-test list`).

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for linear-pp-cli when an agent needs a ranked, joined view across Linear entities (today, bottleneck, blocking, slipped, initiatives health) instead of N separate API calls. It is the right choice for sprint-planning, weekly-update, and triage rituals that compound across cycles and projects. For one-off issue lookups or simple CRUD on a single ticket, the typed `issues get`, `issues update`, and `issues create` commands are also exposed and idempotent.

recipes.title: Daily standup view
recipes.explanation: High-priority work for today, narrowed to a few fields so an agent doesn't drag the full issue payload into context.

recipes.title: Friday stakeholder update
recipes.command: linear-pp-cli cycles compare current previous --json && linear-pp-cli slipped --team ENG --json
recipes.explanation: Cycle-over-cycle metrics plus what carried over, in the shape Maya pastes into her stakeholder doc.

recipes.title: Project landing forecast
recipes.explanation: Predicts completion from measured velocity instead of the static target date the project was created with.

recipes.title: Safe testing on a real workspace
recipes.command: linear-pp-cli config set trust-mode strict && linear-pp-cli issues create --title 'pp-test sanity' --team ENG && linear-pp-cli pp-cleanup --session current
recipes.explanation: Strict trust-mode plus pp-test/pp-cleanup means the CLI only mutates the issues it created — never pre-existing tickets.

recipes.title: Find duplicates before filing
recipes.command: linear-pp-cli similar "login button broken" --json --select identifier,title,score | head
recipes.explanation: FTS5 over the synced corpus; works offline and won't hit the API complexity budget.

trigger_phrases: what should I work on today
who's overloaded on the platform team
compare last cycle to this cycle
